mission.title: Drain a Kubernetes node safely for maintenance
mission.description: Operational runbook for cordoning, draining, validating workload rescheduling, and restoring a node after maintenance. Use it before host patching, kubelet upgrades, or hardware work so workloads move cleanly without leaving the node schedulable.

steps.title: Select the target node and inspect hosted workloads
steps.description: Export NODE_NAME, confirm the node exists, and list the hosted pods that will be affected by the drain.
commands:
: "${NODE_NAME:?Set NODE_NAME to the node you want to drain}"
kubectl get node "$NODE_NAME" -o wide
kubectl get pods -A --field-selector spec.nodeName="$NODE_NAME" -o wide
steps.failureHandling: If the node name is wrong, stop and choose a valid node from kubectl get nodes. If the node is already NotReady, investigate the underlying issue before draining.

steps.title: Cordon the node to stop new scheduling
steps.description: Mark the node unschedulable first so no new workloads land while existing pods are being evicted.
steps.validation: kubectl get node "$NODE_NAME" -o jsonpath='{.spec.unschedulable}' | grep -q true
steps.failureHandling: If cordon fails because of RBAC, use the RBAC audit runbook to grant node maintenance access before continuing.

steps.title: Drain workloads from the node
steps.description: Evict workloads while ignoring DaemonSets and removing emptyDir data only if your maintenance window allows it.
steps.validation: kubectl get node "$NODE_NAME" -o jsonpath='{.spec.unschedulable}' | grep -q true
steps.failureHandling: If drain hangs, inspect PodDisruptionBudget settings and long termination times. Resolve the blocking workload instead of forcing the drain immediately.

steps.title: Verify workloads rescheduled to healthy nodes
steps.description: Check that affected workloads are now running on other nodes and that the drained node remains unschedulable during maintenance.
commands:
kubectl get pods -A -o wide
kubectl get node "$NODE_NAME" -o wide
steps.validation: kubectl get node "$NODE_NAME" -o jsonpath='{.spec.unschedulable}' | grep -q true
steps.failureHandling: If workloads did not reschedule, the cluster may lack spare capacity or a disruption budget may still be blocking progress. Add capacity or relax the budget.

steps.title: Uncordon the node after maintenance completes
steps.description: Return the node to service only after host maintenance, upgrades, or repairs are complete and kubelet health is green again.
steps.validation: kubectl get node "$NODE_NAME" -o jsonpath='{.spec.unschedulable}' | grep -qv true
steps.failureHandling: If the node does not become Ready after uncordon, cordon it again and inspect kubelet, CNI, and disk health before leaving it schedulable.

resolution.summary: The target node was cordoned, drained, verified, and returned to service cleanly. This provides a repeatable node-drain operation for maintenance and upgrade workflows.

troubleshooting.title: Drain is blocked by PodDisruptionBudget
troubleshooting.description: Inspect the affected workload and disruption budget, then scale replicas or adjust the budget temporarily so eviction can proceed without violating availability rules.

troubleshooting.title: DaemonSet pods still appear on the node
troubleshooting.description: That is expected because drain ignores DaemonSet-managed pods. Confirm only DaemonSet or mirror pods remain before starting maintenance.

prerequisites.resources: Sufficient cluster capacity to reschedule workloads away from the drained node.
prerequisites.description: Export NODE_NAME before running the mission. The operator needs permission to cordon, drain, and uncordon nodes.
